hero.headline: Shoolini Online MBA in Biotechnology Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved 2-year programme with Pay After Placement and NAAC A+ accreditation

sections.tldr: Shoolini Online MBA in Biotechnology Management covers biotech product commercialisation, regulatory affairs (CDSCO, DBT), biotech IP management, clinical research operations, bioprocess management, and biopharma business strategy. PAP option Rs 1,50,000 or opt-out Rs 1,30,000. NAAC A+. NIRF #69 University. Shoolini's own biotech research strength adds domain depth.

about.body:
Biotechnology Management sits at the intersection of life sciences and business administration — a combination in acute demand as India's biopharma, agri-biotech, and medical devices sectors scale up. CRISPR-based agri products, biosimilar manufacturing, vaccine production, and medtech all require managers who understand both the science and the commercialisation pathway.

Shoolini University Online's MBA in Biotechnology Management covers biotech product commercialisation pathways, CDSCO and DBT regulatory framework for biologics and biosimilars, biotechnology intellectual property and patent landscape, clinical research operations management, bioprocess and GMP compliance management, biopharma value chain analysis, and biotech startup financing. The programme is designed for life sciences graduates, pharma and biotech company professionals, and clinical research associates targeting management transitions.

Shoolini University has a strong biotech research programme on its campus — the university's research output in bioinformatics, plant biotechnology, and pharmaceutical sciences is nationally ranked. This institutional depth in biotech science gives Shoolini's Biotechnology Management MBA a domain-grounded academic context that most online universities lack.

Shoolini holds NIRF #69 (University, 2025) and NAAC A+ accreditation. PAP available. EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 subjects are shared across all Shoolini MBA specialisations. Sem 3-4 below are specific to the Biotechnology Management specialisation.

subjects.name: Legal Aspects of Business
subjects.desc: Business contracts, corporate governance, consumer protection obligations, and regulatory compliance frameworks for managers.

subjects.name: Introductory Biotech and Microbiology
subjects.desc: Cell biology fundamentals, microbial applications in industry, biotech product categories, and life sciences sector overview.

subjects.name: Industrial and Micro Biotechnology
subjects.desc: Fermentation process management, enzyme technology, biosimilar manufacturing basics, and industrial biotech application areas.

subjects.name: Storytelling with Data
subjects.desc: Visual communication of data findings, chart selection, and translating analytics into business narratives for non-technical audiences.

subjects.name: Acing Interviews through AI
subjects.desc: AI-assisted mock interview practice, personal brand communication, and job-search strategy frameworks for management graduates.

subjects.name: Intro to Editing
subjects.desc: Professional editing skills for business documents, reports, presentations, and digital content.

subjects.name: Future Leader's Program
subjects.desc: Executive presence building, stakeholder influence, high-performance team leadership, and personal effectiveness frameworks for emerging managers.

subjects.name: Sales Management
subjects.desc: Sales force structure, territory planning, pipeline management, forecasting, and sales performance incentive design.

subjects.name: Regulatory Affairs in Biotech
subjects.desc: CDSCO biologics approval pathway, Schedule M GMP for biologics, biosimilar dossier requirements, and DBT GMO approval process.

subjects.name: Entrepreneurship in Life Sciences
subjects.desc: Biotech startup formation, BIRAC grant application process, IP licensing strategy, and life sciences venture funding frameworks.

subjects.name: Capstone Project
subjects.desc: Applied industry project integrating specialisation knowledge with structured problem-solving for a real-world business challenge.

whoHires.body:
Biotechnology Management MBA graduates from Shoolini University Online are hired across India's life sciences and biotech sector:

**Industries:** Biopharma and biosimilar manufacturers, clinical research organisations (CROs), agri-biotech companies, medical devices, healthcare analytics, DBT and DST funded institutions, biotech startups, and CDMO (contract development and manufacturing) companies

**Entry-level roles:** Regulatory Affairs Associate, Clinical Research Coordinator, Biotech Business Development Associate, IP Analyst (biotech), Bioprocess Executive, Science Writer (biopharma)

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Regulatory Affairs Manager, Clinical Operations Manager, Biotech Product Manager, IP Portfolio Manager, Bioprocess Quality Manager, Business Development Manager (CDMO)

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Regulatory Affairs, Head of Clinical Operations, Director Biotech Business Development, Chief IP Officer, VP Manufacturing (biologics), Country Manager (biotech MNC)

**Top hiring organisations:** Biocon, Dr. Reddy's Laboratories, Serum Institute of India, Cipla Biologics, Zydus Biologics, Panacea Biotec, Bharat Biotech, IQVIA India, Syneos Health, Icon Plc India, Parexel India, and biotech startups in the Chandigarh-Mohali BioPark and Bengaluru biotech cluster

skills.body:
The Biotechnology Management specialisation builds skills that biopharma, CRO, and biotech companies test at manager and analyst levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Regulatory Affairs: CDSCO biologics approval pathway, Schedule M GMP requirements for biologics, DBT approval for GMO research, biosimilar regulatory pathway under New Drugs and Clinical Trials Rules 2019, and ICMR ethical review process
- Biotech IP: patent claim drafting basics, biotech patent landscape search (Espacenet, Indian Patent Office), technology licensing agreement structure, and university-industry technology transfer frameworks
- Clinical Research Management: ICH-GCP compliance basics, clinical trial protocol review, IRB/IEC submission process, site management organisation (SMO) operations, and clinical data management overview
- Bioprocess Management: upstream and downstream bioprocess overview, GMP documentation for biologics, batch record review, and QA audit readiness for FDA/EMA inspections

**Applied skills:**
- Biotech Commercialisation: product development-to-launch financial modelling, go-to-market for diagnostic kits, hospital formulary access strategy, and price negotiation with government tenders (GEM portal)
- Startup Financing: DBT BIRAC grant application process, DPIIT biotechnology startup registration, Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC) CRS scheme, and VC pitch for biotech early-stage companies

**Soft skills:** Translating regulatory risk to business stakeholders, managing cross-functional teams with both scientific and commercial objectives, presenting IP strategy to board, and communicating clinical trial results in business language.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Shoolini Online MBA Biotechnology Management against peer programmes:

**No direct UGC-DEB online MBA peer**: As of 2026, no other UGC-DEB approved online MBA university lists Biotechnology Management as a standalone specialisation. Amity, NMIMS, CU, MUJ, LPU, JAIN, UPES, and SMU do not offer dedicated Biotechnology Management MBAs online. Shoolini is the only option for biotech professionals wanting a UGC-DEB entitled online management degree with biotechnology domain depth on the marksheet.

**vs TERI SAS M.Sc. Biotechnology Management (campus):** TERI School of Advanced Studies offers a campus-based programme at New Delhi combining biology, policy, and management for sustainability-focused biotech roles. This requires full-time campus attendance. For working biotech professionals, Shoolini's online MBA provides the recognised degree without career disruption.

**vs MBA with pharma/biotech electives at campus MBA programmes:** IIM Ahmedabad Life Sciences Club, ISB's health management electives, and Great Lakes' biotech electives cater to an entirely different candidate profile. These are full-time, high-fee campus programmes. For working biotech professionals wanting a management degree credential without career interruption, Shoolini's online MBA at Rs 1,30,000 with PAP is the accessible route.

fees.body:
The Shoolini University Online MBA in Biotechnology Management carries a regular fee of Rs 2,00,000 total across 4 semesters (2-year programme). A Rs 500 one-time application fee applies. No registration fee, no exam fees, no project fees beyond the programme fee.

**Two offer tracks**:

**Offer 1 - MBA with Pay After Placement (PAP)**:
- Rs 50,000 scholarship applied. Effective fee Rs 1,50,000 (25% savings).
- Payment split: Rs 1,20,000 before placement (Rs 30,000 x 4 semesters) + Rs 30,000 payable after confirmed placement.

**Offer 2 - MBA Opt-out from PAP**:
- Rs 70,000 scholarship applied. Effective fee Rs 1,30,000 (35% savings).
- Payment: Rs 32,500 x 4 semesters.

**Merit scholarship (10% additional)**:
For candidates scoring 90% or above in Class 12 (UG entry) or 90% or above in UG degree. Non-combinable with PAP or opt-out tracks, stackable with lump-sum and annual discounts.

**Additional payment discounts (stackable with merit only)**:
- 10% off on full course fee with single lump-sum payment
- Additional 5% off on annual payment in full

**Zero-cost EMI**: Available directly through Shoolini University (not via external marketing partners or NBFCs). Contact our counsellor for current tenure options.

Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Shoolini each quarter. Please reconfirm with our counsellor before any payment.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive a Shoolini University MBA degree certificate with Biotechnology Management noted on the marksheet, issued under UGC-DEB 2020 online mode standards. This is the only UGC-DEB entitled online MBA degree in India with Biotechnology Management on the marksheet — positioning graduates uniquely for regulatory affairs, clinical operations, and biotech business development roles.

Shoolini's institutional strength in biotech research adds credibility to the degree at India's biopharma and CRO employers. DigiLocker verification is available.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from Shoolini University Online MBA Biotechnology Management alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Priyanka S.
items.liked: CDSCO biologics regulatory pathway and GMP documentation subjects were directly applicable to my CRO work
items.disliked: BIRAC grant application process content was brief — a dedicated module would help biotech startup candidates
items.body: I work as a regulatory affairs executive at a CRO in Mohali. The New Drugs and Clinical Trials Rules 2019 content, biosimilar approval pathway, and GMP audit readiness subjects were directly applicable. No other online MBA offers this combination — Shoolini's research strength in biotech gave the regulatory subjects academic depth. KPMG certification helped in client presentations at regulated industry events. PAP structure reduced upfront commitment while I confirmed my RA Manager promotion.

items.name: Ashok M.
items.liked: Biotech IP and patent landscape subjects were practical for my technology licensing role
items.disliked: More depth on BIRAC and DBT grant programme mechanics would help candidates at biotech startups and incubators
items.body: I work in technology licensing at a Delhi-based biotech incubator. The patent claim basics, technology transfer framework, and licensing agreement structure subjects were directly applicable to the deals I manage. No standard online MBA covers biotech IP in this way. Opted for Rs 1,30,000 opt-out track with annual discount. NAAC A+ from Shoolini met the DPIIT-recognised startup's educational credential requirement for the Senior Manager designation.

items.name: Kavya R.
items.liked: Clinical research operations and ICH-GCP basics were directly relevant to my CRA role transition
items.disliked: Placement support in Hyderabad's large pharma cluster was limited compared to Shoolini's North India network
items.body: I transitioned from a clinical research associate to a clinical operations coordinator using this MBA. The ICH-GCP compliance, IRB submission process, and site management organisation operations subjects gave me the management framework I needed for the step up. Shoolini's biotech research reputation gave the degree added credibility at CRO employers who ask about academic background. The Rs 1,30,000 fee was competitive compared to standalone CRA-to-management training programmes.

faqs.question: Why does Shoolini's biotech research strength matter for this MBA?
faqs.answer: Shoolini University has nationally recognised campus research programmes in bioinformatics, plant biotechnology, pharmaceutical sciences, and molecular biology. This institutional depth means Biotechnology Management MBA students have access to faculty with active research backgrounds, making the regulatory affairs, bioprocess, and IP subjects more domain-grounded than a generic online MBA with a biotech elective.

faqs.question: Is this MBA suitable for a science graduate with no prior management experience?
faqs.answer: Yes. The Sem 1-2 common core builds all foundational management competencies — Financial Accounting, Marketing Management, Organisational Behaviour, and Financial Management — from scratch. The Sem 3-4 Biotechnology Management specialisation then applies these to biotech industry contexts. Life sciences graduates from B.Sc., B.Tech. Biotech, or B.Pharm programmes are the target profile.

faqs.question: What CDSCO regulatory content is covered for biologics and biosimilars?
faqs.answer: The Biotechnology Management specialisation covers the New Drugs and Clinical Trials Rules 2019 biologics approval pathway, Schedule M GMP requirements for biologics manufacturing, biosimilar approval dossier structure, DBT approval process for GMO research products, and ICMR ethical review requirements. These are directly applicable for regulatory affairs manager roles at biopharma companies and CROs.

faqs.question: Is the Shoolini Biotech Management MBA valid for CDMO and biosimilar manufacturer roles?
faqs.answer: Yes. The UGC-DEB entitled, NAAC A+ degree satisfies management degree requirements at CDMOs, biosimilar manufacturers, and biopharma companies for regulatory affairs manager, bioprocess quality manager, and business development manager designations. The Biotechnology Management specialisation's GMP and regulatory subjects are directly relevant to CDMO audit and compliance roles.

faqs.question: Is there a BIRAC or DBT grant option for students enrolled in biotech management?
faqs.answer: The programme itself does not come with BIRAC funding. However, the curriculum covers BIRAC CRS scheme mechanics, DPIIT biotech startup registration, and DBT grant application processes as part of the biotech startup financing subject. This gives students the knowledge to apply for BIRAC grants for their own biotech ventures independently after graduation.

chooseIf: You are a life sciences graduate or biotech professional wanting the only UGC-DEB online MBA with Biotechnology Management on the marksheet.
You want CDSCO regulatory, biotech IP, and clinical research operations content alongside a recognised management degree.
Your budget is Rs 1,30,000 with Pay After Placement and Shoolini's institutional biotech research depth.
You cannot attend a campus biotech management programme due to work commitments.
consider: You can attend a full-time campus programme and want TERI SAS's sustainability-focused biotech management credential.
You want a higher NIRF-ranked online university for a general MBA with pharma or healthcare electives.
You specifically need clinical research management from a CRO-affiliated institution with stronger Hyderabad pharma placement networks.
